entities.name: ABIOTIC STRESS
entities.description: Non-living environmental factors that trigger adaptive responses in plants

entities.name: SIGNAL TRANSDUCTION
entities.description: The process of transmitting signals from the cell surface to the nucleus to initiate a response

entities.name: ADAPTIVE INNATE RESPONSES
entities.description: The inherent biological reactions of plants to adapt to adverse environmental conditions

entities.name: PLANT PERSISTENCE
entities.description: The ability of plants to survive and persist under adverse environmental conditions

entities.name: PLANTS
entities.description: Multicellular organisms that respond to abiotic stress to ensure cell survival

entities.name: HEAT SHOCK PROTEINS
entities.description: Proteins that maintain cellular homeostasis and preserve nonnative proteins in a competent state

entities.name: CELLULAR HOMEOSTASIS
entities.description: The maintenance of a stable internal environment within plant cells

entities.name: HSP70/90
entities.description: Specific heat shock proteins involved in complex stress response mechanisms

entities.name: ATPASE-COUPLED CONFORMATIONAL MODIFICATIONS
entities.description: Mechanisms involving ATPase that alter protein structure for functional activity

entities.name: COCHAPERONES
entities.description: Proteins that interact with heat shock proteins to assist in protein remodeling

entities.name: MAP KINASES
entities.description: Signaling proteins that interact with heat shock proteins

entities.name: CA2+ REGULATED PROTEINS
entities.description: Proteins regulated by calcium ions that interact with heat shock proteins

entities.name: HSFS
entities.description: Heat shock factors that interact with heat shock proteins in signaling pathways

entities.name: REACTIVE OXYGEN SPECIES
entities.description: Chemically reactive oxygen-containing molecules that interact with heat shock proteins
